title: Add explicit less-than constraint for expiry checks in AgentPolicy
description: AgentPolicy performs Num2Bits(64) range checks on expiryTimestamp and currentTimestamp but the circuit source shown is truncated and may lack an explicit LessThan(64) constraint proving currentTimestamp < expiryTimestamp. If this comparison is missing or only checked in Solidity, an attacker can generate a proof with an expired credential and submit it before the contract-side check (or the contract may not re-derive expiry from public signals). Add a LessThan(64) component inside the circuit: isNotExpired.in[0] <== currentTimestamp; isNotExpired.in[1] <== expiryTimestamp; isNotExpired.out === 1. Similarly verify the Delegation circuit enforces delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry inside the SNARK, not just in the contract. Constraint cost is ~130 per LessThan(64).
